name: Yang Zhu
page_title: Yang Zhu 楊竺 [Wu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives

sections.short-biography: One of the chief supporters of Sun Ba. Slandered Wu Can.

sections.biography-source-undefined:
Yang Zhu was from Guangling commandery.

He obtained fame when he was young, but some like Lu Xun predicted he would suffer failure.

After the heir to the Wu empire, Sun Deng, died in 241, Sun Quan made Sun He the heir. However, Sun Ba also had a good claim to being the heir, and factions formed in favor of either two. Yang Zhu belonged to the Sun Ba faction. He privately told Sun Quan that Sun Ba was talented in both the government and military, and that Sun Ba should be the successor. He and Sun Ba also slandered Wu Can of the Sun He faction, and in 245, Sun Quan executed Wu Can. He also slandered Lu Xun, citing twenty offenses, but this got nowhere. Sun Quan was becoming increasingly irritated with the factional turmoil and in 250, he forced Sun Ba to commit suicide and deposed Sun He. In the aftermath, Yang Zhu was imprisoned and tortured by Sun Quan. He was then promptly executed and his corpse cast into a river. His family was exiled to the south.

full_text:
Yang Zhu 楊竺
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Yang Zhu (楊竺)was born in Guangling commandery. One of the chief supporters of Sun Ba. Slandered Wu Can. Yang Zhu was affiliated with the Wu Kingdom. Yang Zhu lived during the Three Kingdoms period of Chinese history.

BiographySource Undefined
Yang Zhu was from Guangling commandery.
He obtained fame when he was young, but some like Lu Xun predicted he would suffer failure.
After the heir to the Wu empire, Sun Deng, died in 241, Sun Quan made Sun He the heir. However, Sun Ba also had a good claim to being the heir, and factions formed in favor of either two. Yang Zhu belonged to the Sun Ba faction. He privately told Sun Quan that Sun Ba was talented in both the government and military, and that Sun Ba should be the successor. He and Sun Ba also slandered Wu Can of the Sun He faction, and in 245, Sun Quan executed Wu Can. He also slandered Lu Xun, citing twenty offenses, but this got nowhere. Sun Quan was becoming increasingly irritated with the factional turmoil and in 250, he forced Sun Ba to commit suicide and deposed Sun He. In the aftermath, Yang Zhu was imprisoned and tortured by Sun Quan. He was then promptly executed and his corpse cast into a river. His family was exiled to the south.
